artPrompt: An ongoing crooked enchantment over a felt gaming table: a pair of loaded dice frozen mid-air glowing faintly with sinister warmth, a goblin's clawed hand hovering to nudge their fate, a small treasure coin and a folded favor exchanged in the gloom below. A single amber lamp pools light on the dice while cigar smoke and ink-black shadow swallow the room. Rendered as a moody oil painting — old-master chiaroscuro, visible impasto brushwork, deep shadow, warm varnished tones.
name: Fix the Dice for a Price
text:
Whenever a player is about to roll one or more dice, you may offer them a deal: they create a Treasure for you and you create a Favor token, OR you sacrifice a Favor token and set the die to any result you like before it's rolled. Loaded dice, every time, for whoever's paid up.
The goblins file the corners off in the back room. It is delicate work. They are not delicate goblins.
flavorText: "House dice," the goblin says, and they are not your dice anymore.
